constraints.special: C=64 is compile-time constant (known at kernel generation)

analysis: When C is runtime-variable, compiler cannot unroll the channel loop
Each iteration requires bounds checking: if (c + kAccessSize <= C)
Register allocation is conservative - cannot guarantee C fits in registers
schedule_reasoning: kFixedChannels iterator hardcodes C at compile time - loop fully unrolled
Compiler knows exact register usage: C=64 with bf16 = 32 registers for channel tile
No runtime bounds checks - predication eliminated entirely
Best for first conv layer where C=3 (RGB) or fixed feature dimensions
concept: Fixed channels iterator - compile-time channel specialization

constraints.special: C=4 is very small (< 32), standard iterator wastes bandwidth

analysis: Tensor core MMA expects K-dim >= 32 for int8, but C=4 means only 4 channels
Standard iterator pads C to 32, loading 28 zeros per 32-byte fetch
For RGB input (C=3), this is even worse: 90.6% bandwidth waste
schedule_reasoning: kFewChannels iterator reorganizes memory access for small C
Packs multiple spatial positions (H,W) into the channel dimension for MMA
Instead of loading C=4 with 28 padding, loads 8 spatial positions × 4 channels = 32
Alignment=4 matches the actual channel count, eliminating padding
Critical for first-layer inference: ResNet input is 224×224×3
concept: Few channels iterator - efficient handling of C < 32

constraints.special: C = alpha*(A*B^T + B*A^T) + beta*C, C is symmetric (lower fill)

analysis: Rank-2K has structure: result is symmetric by construction (A*B^T + B*A^T)
Naive approach computes both triangles then stores only one - 50% compute waste
Two separate GEMMs require 2× memory traffic for A and B
Temporary N×N matrix needed - O(N²) extra memory
schedule_reasoning: SYR2K fuses both GEMM operations into single kernel pass
Tile scheduler only computes lower triangle tiles - 50% compute saved
A and B loaded once, used for both A*B^T and B*A^T terms
No temporary storage - result accumulated directly into C
For Cholesky-based solvers where C = A*A^T + B*B^T pattern is common

constraints.special: C = alpha*A*A^H + beta*C, C is Hermitian (upper fill)

analysis: Hermitian matrix: C[i,j] = conj(C[j,i]), diagonal is real
A^H (conjugate transpose) requires conjugation during the transpose
SYRK assumes C[i,j] = C[j,i] (symmetric), HERK has conjugate relationship
Interleaved complex (real,imag,real,imag) prevents efficient tensor core use
schedule_reasoning: HERK kernel handles conjugate transpose internally via ComplexTransform::kConjugate
FillMode::kUpper stores upper triangle; lower is implicit conjugate mirror
Tile scheduler skips lower triangle tiles entirely
Diagonal tiles special-cased: diagonal elements forced to real (imag=0)
Used in covariance matrices, quantum mechanics, signal processing
concept: HERK - Hermitian rank-k for complex covariance

constraints.special: C = alpha*A*B + beta*C, A is Hermitian (lower fill), left side

analysis: Hermitian A stored as lower triangle; upper accessed via conjugate of lower
Per-element conjugation during upper triangle access causes divergence
No recognition that A*B with Hermitian A has special structure
schedule_reasoning: HEMM with SideMode::kLeft computes C = A*B where A is Hermitian
Tile loader reads lower triangle and applies conjugate for upper portion
No expansion: A tiles loaded once, mirrored in shared memory
FillMode::kLower tells loader which triangle is physically stored
Common in physics simulations, quantum computing, spectral methods
concept: HEMM - Hermitian matrix multiply for complex physics
